message:
fix(tools): reset SIGPIPE to SIG_DFL in spawned children to prevent bash-tool hangs

Pi's async runtime (asupersync, like tokio) installs `SIGPIPE = SIG_IGN`
at startup so internal pipe-write failures inside the runtime don't
tear the agent down. POSIX inherits signal dispositions across `exec
(2)`, so every child pi spawns inherits `SIG_IGN` for SIGPIPE.

When pi later closes its read end of the child's stdout (output
truncation, agent timeout, shutdown), the child no longer dies —
every `write(2)` returns `EPIPE` and most well-behaved CLI tools
(`fd`, `ls`, GNU coreutils, etc.) treat `EPIPE` as a recoverable I/O
error and keep going. Result: the child loops forever, the parent
blocks on `wait4(2)`, the bash tool hangs.

Reset `SIGPIPE` to `SIG_DFL` in the forked child via
`Command::pre_exec` before `execve(2)` runs:

```rust
unsafe {
    command.pre_exec(|| {
        libc::signal(libc::SIGPIPE, libc::SIG_DFL);
        Ok(())
    });
}
```

This gives the kernel the standard "writer dies on broken pipe"
behavior every Unix CLI tool implicitly relies on, so closing the
read end of the child's stdout from pi's side actually terminates
the child.

Notes:

- The reset happens in `pre_exec`, which the standard library runs
  between `fork(2)` and `exec(2)` in the child. Only async-signal-
  safe calls are allowed there. `signal(2)` is on POSIX's async-
  signal-safe list (POSIX.1 § 2.4.3), which is exactly the contract
  `pre_exec` requires.
- We restore the platform default; we do not save and restore a
  user handler. There is no user handler to save in pi's case
  (asupersync only installs `SIG_IGN`, not a custom handler).

Crate-level lint configuration relaxes `unsafe_code = "forbid"` →
`"deny"` (in both `Cargo.toml` and the crate root attribute) so a
single, narrowly-scoped `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` on
`isolate_command_process_group` is permitted. All other crate code
is unchanged. The deny level still produces a hard error for any
unsafe block that doesn't carry the explicit allow attribute, so
this is functionally equivalent to forbid for everything except the
one annotated function.

`libc = "0.2"` is added to the workspace dependency list under the
existing "Signals" section (alongside `ctrlc`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -159,6 +159,7 @@ url = "2"
 
 # Signals
 ctrlc = "3.5.2"
+libc = "0.2"
 
 # Serialization
 serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive", "rc"] }
@@ -313,7 +314,12 @@ debug = 1
 strip = false
 
 [lints.rust]
-unsafe_code = "forbid"
+# `deny` rather than `forbid` so a single, narrowly-scoped
+# `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` is permitted on `isolate_command_process_group`
+# in `src/tools.rs` — it needs `Command::pre_exec` to reset SIGPIPE in
+# child processes (see SAFETY comment there). All other crate code is
+# unchanged.
+unsafe_code = "deny"
 
 [lints.clippy]
 pedantic = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }
